title: Stories Generator Output
description: Output schema for the stories generator agent. Stories are the smallest unit of delivery — each represents a single implementable capability that a developer can complete within one sprint. Stories follow 'As a / I want / So that' format with Given/When/Then acceptance criteria, ordered by implementation tier (Foundation → Core → Enhancement → Polish).

items.description: Array of user stories ordered by tier within each feature

execution_summary.description: Plain-text summary: story count per tier, recommended sprint sequence, effort estimate, and reflection findings

Story.description: A single implementable user story. Represents one developer's work within one sprint. Must be independently testable and deliverable.

epic_id.description: Parent epic this story belongs to

tier.description: Implementation tier: 1=Foundation (setup, data models, SDK integration), 2=Core (happy path, primary UI), 3=Enhancement (validations, error handling, edge cases), 4=Polish (performance, UX refinement). Lower tiers implemented first.

title.description: Story title in 'As a <role>, I want <capability> so that <benefit>' format

acceptance_criteria.description: Testable acceptance criteria in 'Given <context>, When <action>, Then <outcome>' format. Must be specific enough for QA to write tests from.

story_points.description: Complexity estimate. Max 5 — if larger, story must be split. 1=trivial config, 2=simple CRUD, 3=moderate logic, 5=complex integration/multi-component.

data_sensitivity.description: Data classification. Public=catalog/rates/terms. Internal=app status/config. Confidential=name/email/employment. Restricted=national ID/income/biometric.

depends_on.description: Story IDs this story depends on. Tier 1 (Foundation) stories within the same feature must have no intra-feature dependencies. Cross-feature dependencies allowed.

implements.description: FR IDs this story implements. Traces back to original requirements via feature → epic → FR chain.

StoryMetadata.description: Reasoning, confidence, citation, and decision trajectory explaining why this story exists in this form

confidence.description: Self-assessed confidence. 0.9+ for stories directly from explicit feature acceptance summaries. 0.7-0.9 for inferred decompositions.

reasoning.description: Explains: why this tier, why these story points, why this sequence position, what it depends on and why

citation.description: Traces this story back to its source feature

source_reference.description: Feature ID (e.g., FEAT-01-01) or FR ID that this story derives from

trajectory.description: Decision-making steps showing how this story was derived from the parent feature
